name: Homepage pop-up and hover reactions
description: Fix non-functional pop-up animations and hover/click reactions on the homepage. Ensure all interactive elements respond correctly to user interactions.

name: Full responsive layout — homepage
description: Ensure the homepage is fully responsive across all breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop). Test and fix any layout overflow, wrapping, or scaling issues.

name: Full responsive layout — global
description: Audit and fix responsive layouts across the entire application, not just the homepage. Every page must render correctly on mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.

name: Account page — username management
description: Replace the placeholder account page with a functional form allowing users to update their display name and username. Include validation and save confirmation.

name: Account page — plan management
description: Add a plan section to the account page showing the user's current plan (Free or Pro) with an upgrade/downgrade flow. Connect to Stripe when the payment feature is live.

name: Conference multi-day scheduling (up to 5 days)
description: Allow conference organizers to plan a conference spanning 1–5 days. Each day has its own timeline where slots (talks, breaks, workshops, etc.) can be created, moved, and resized.

name: Conference slot management
description: Organizers can create named, typed reserved slots (talk, break, keynote, workshop, lightning talk, etc.) within each day. Slots have a start time, duration, title, and optional description.

name: Speaker assignment to conference slots
description: Organizers can assign speakers to slots by typing an email address. Each assigned speaker receives an email invitation to join the conference. Affiliation grants the speaker temporary Pro access scoped to the event window.

name: Speaker conference talk portal
description: Speakers manage personal + conference talks from the dashboard: a 'Conference Talks' section (getSpeakerConferenceTalks) lists slots assigned via accepted affiliations with conference name, dates, slot time, and a status badge, excluded from the free-tier count and read-only after the event ends. Shipped and browser-verified.

name: Stripe payment integration
description: Integrate Stripe to handle plan upgrades, subscription management, and any paid features. Includes: DB schema additions, /api/stripe/checkout, /api/stripe/webhook (checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated/deleted, invoice.payment_failed), /api/locale for currency detection, usePaywall() context + PaywallModal, updated account page with event date picker for one-time plan, updated landing page pricing section.

subtasks.name: Plan catalog with inline price_data (EUR/USD) — no static products needed
Account page: real plan display + event date picker + manage link

name: Color mode (theme switcher)
description: Light mode implemented alongside dark via CSS-variable tokens: .light overrides on <html>, .force-dark pins live stage screens (wall, present, speaker, remote, qna, app, demo) dark, and an anti-FOUC inline script applies the theme pre-paint. Sun/moon toggle defaults to system and persists to localStorage. Whole-app token migration done (38 homepage hex colors + 28 themed files).

name: Favicon — SVG with 'e' in jsconf colors
description: Replace the placeholder favicon with a custom SVG favicon displaying the letter 'e' styled in jsconf brand colors. Provide both SVG and ICO fallback formats.

name: PowerPoint and PDF presentation support
description: Client-side extraction via pdfjs-dist (PDF rendering) + jszip (PPTX image extraction). Extracted slides are uploaded to Vercel Blob as a manifest JSON. Presenter renders extracted images with prev/next navigation in /present/[sessionId].

name: iFrame URL embedding
description: Generalized form input to accept any embeddable URL (Slides.com, Google Slides, Figma, Miro, etc.). Presenter view renders the URL as an iframe. No experimental disclaimer needed — widely supported.

name: Speaker slide control via phone
description: Web phone remote at /remote/[token]; speaker-scoped JWT minted at /api/remote/token with 8h expiry. Remote broadcasts slide_next/slide_prev over PartyKit. Deployed to jsconf-live-wall.salvadorsc.partykit.dev with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET set.

name: Q&A — flagged questions remain visible (dimmed)
description: Flagged questions are filtered out for attendees but broadcast to moderators, who see them dimmed with a 'Flagged' badge sorted to the bottom. Implemented server-side and deployed to jsconf-live-wall.salvadorsc.partykit.dev.

name: Q&A — question deletion by moderator
description: Moderators can permanently delete individual questions from the Q&A view with a confirmation step. Enforced admin-only on the PartyKit server and broadcast to all clients in realtime. Deployed.

name: Q&A — ban user on question ban
description: Moderators can ban a question's author (by stable authorId) for the rest of the session. Server removes the author's questions and rejects further submissions; bans are in-memory and session-scoped. Deployed.

name: Dev floating overlay — testing tracker
description: A draggable floating panel visible only in development (NODE_ENV=development). Repurposed from a dev-completion checklist into a manual Testing Tracker: every feature (grouped by phase) and its subtasks cycle untested -> pass -> fail on click, with pass/fail/untested counts, a two-color progress bar, per-phase failing badges, and a reset button, plus a read-only Ideas tab for reference. Status persists in localStorage under 'testtracker:status'. No effect on production UI.

name: Pro waitlist — email capture from landing page
description: Store visitor emails from the 'Notify me' button on the Pro pricing card of the landing page. Emails are saved to the pro_waitlist table in Supabase. Duplicate emails return a graceful message. Once the Pro plan is live via Stripe, the list is used to contact interested users.

name: Call for Papers (CFP)
description: Public CFP submission page (/cfp/[slug]) per conference with fixed core fields + organizer custom questions (text/long_text/select/multi_select/checkbox), honeypot + per-email cap. Organizer settings (open/close window, question builder) at /dashboard/conference/[id]/cfp and a review dashboard (filter, 1-5 rating, notes, accept/reject/waitlist). Accept creates an unscheduled slot + pending affiliation (reuses Phase 2). Transactional email via Resend (lib/email.ts) for accept/reject/waitlist, which also backfills the Phase 2 invite stub; sends are best-effort and no-op without RESEND_API_KEY. See docs/phase-7-call-for-papers.md.

name: Q&A history — persisted to dashboard
description: Ending a presenter session snapshots the live PartyKit questions to the Supabase questions table via /api/sessions/snapshot-questions (admin client, ownership-checked, best-effort). A new dashboard view at /dashboard/sessions/[sessionId]/questions reads them back (sorted by votes, answered state shown), making the homepage 'saved to your dashboard' FAQ claim true. The talk detail page links ended sessions to 'Q&A History' and live ones to the live view.

name: Dedicated pricing page (Slido-style)
description: Public /pricing page with an audience toggle (Speakers / Organizers). Speakers see Free (EUR 0) + Speaker Pro (monthly/annual toggle, 'Coming soon' badge + waitlist, no checkout). Organizers see the three live tiers (one-time / Growth / Scale) with currency-aware prices from /api/locale and a 'Popular' highlight. Linked from the homepage nav and pricing teaser. Fully themed for light/dark. Pricing model captured in docs/pricing.md.
